_note: Ambient advisor counsel per management screen. When you pin a footer portrait OFF the scene page, they speak to THIS screen. `lean` gates a line to a personality pole (bold/cautious, devout/worldly, fiery/cool) or a competence (strong/wise/cunning/brave) or a school/class; untagged lines fit anyone. `if` gates a line to a pressure state (same condition vocabulary as scenes, e.g. {"lte":["provisions",25]}); when any `if` line is currently true it takes PRIORITY — the advisor raises the crisis. The line is cast in the member's voice — {name}/{they}/{class}/{school} tokens work.

coven.text: Faith is the only wall that never falls. Tend the coven's heart before its stones.
Sentiment won't fill a belly. Keep them fed and they'll keep the faith.
A coven grows by daring. Send me where the danger is — the others will follow.
Guard who we have. Every Wizard lost is a rune we never chant again.
Half these apprentices have never bled for the hold. That should change.
Their faith is guttering like the hearth-rune. Mend it now, or we lose them one by one.
The coven would follow you into the Flame itself. Don't spend that trust lightly.
We are what remains of the old Circle. Lead us well, and we hold.

fields.text: The stores won't stretch to the thaw. We plant now, or we starve in it.
Let me lead a forage into the deep wastes. I'll come back heavy, or not at all.
Bless the fields. The Flame has fed us through darker springs than this.
Buy seed-grain while the Reach still trades. Coin keeps better than hope.
Drive the harvest hard. Backs will ache, but bellies won't.
The stores are nearly bare. This is no season for half-measures — we plant or forage now.
We're a bad week from famine. Whatever you mean to do, do it this season.
A coven cannot eat runes. Mind the stores before they mind us.

market.text: Coin is the one magic the Gilded Reach respects. Spend it before it spends us.
Don't owe the Reach a favour. Their debts come due in blood.
I can turn this coin twice before the factors notice. Let me do the dealing.
Trade if we must — but do not kneel to aristocrats who forgot the Flame.
Buy Red's favour now. We'll want a friend with a full purse before long.
The purse is near empty. We've little left to trade our way out of trouble.
Give the coin a job, or it does nothing at all.

war.text: Raid the caravans. Steel fears the magic that strikes first.
Every raid stirs the Fracture. Fortify — don't provoke what we can't unmake.
Hire mercenaries and let them bleed for us. I'd sooner cast than swing a blade.
The coven distrusts steel it cannot enchant. Do not lean on hired swords.
Muster the coven. If the Warriors come, let them find us ready.
The ash is at the walls. Fortify, or the Fracture ends this hold before any Warrior does.
Magic against steel — choose the ground before they choose it for you.

workings.text: My hands are steady. Give me the hard working and stand well back.
Deepen the runes while I still remember them. The Forgetting takes the old spells first.
Court the Flame if you dare — but send someone whose soul you can spare.
Mend the wards first. A working undone is cheaper than a hold unmade.
Match the caster to the colour. Waste no mana on a spell that fights the hand that casts it.
We're all but dry. One working, chosen well — and no more this season.
Mend the wards before anything else. The Fracture is close enough to feel now.
The old runes are slipping from us. Deepen them while any of us still remember them.
Mana sits idle. Put it to work before it's needed and gone.

cults.text: Choose your friends among the seven with care. Every ally is another's enemy.
A cult wronged remembers longer than a cult befriended. Mind your feuds.
Play the colours against each other. We're too small to fight them, big enough to tip them.
The Blue Bastion still keeps the old lore. Court them before the Forgetting wins it.
Let them circle. A hold that flinches at every cult never grows.
Seven cults, and no pleasing all of them at once. Choose.

map.text: The Secret Tower waits out there in the dark. Someone must dare the road to it.
Every hold on that map can become a grave. Scout before you stride.
All roads bend back to the Tower and its Flame. That is the only road that matters.
The world forgets itself a little more each season. Map it while it still holds a shape.
Look how the ash has eaten the edges. Soon there'll be no map left to read.
The Flame leans toward us. The Tower is nearer now than it has ever been — press on.
Runehold at the centre, the ash at the edge. Watch which of them grows.

saga.text: What we do is written whether we will it or not. Give the chroniclers worthy ink.
The saga keeps no lies. Read it when you doubt the road we've walked.
Let them write that Runehold dared. Better a bright saga than a long grey one.
If this is to be our last chapter, let it read that we did not go quietly.
Every choice becomes a line in this. Make it a saga worth the telling.
